question.content: In the laser Doppler velocimetry setup described, write the expression for the photodetector output current. Answer format: a single algebraic expression in SingleExpr form. Use the symbols E_0, phi_1, phi_2, v, alpha, beta, and the other symbols defined in the problem; use ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Superpose the two scattered electric fields from the same particle.
step_1.key_point: The detector current follows the interference intensity.

step_3.content: Use particle motion to make the relative phase time-dependent.
step_3.key_point: The beat term comes from the phase difference evolution.

step_5.content: Project velocity on the wave-vector difference direction.
step_5.key_point: Delta omega is set by (k_1-k_2) dot v.

step_7.content: Substitute the beat angular frequency into the interference-current form.
step_7.key_point: Keep one compact final expression only.
